title: Functional managers and directors n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Plan, direct, or coordinate marketing policies and programs, such as determining the demand for products and services offered by a firm and its competitors, and identify potential customers. Develop pricing strategies with the goal of maximizing the firm's profits or share of the market while ensuring the firm's customers are satisfied. Oversee product development or monitor trends that indicate the need for new products and services. Plan, direct, or coordinate the actual distribution or movement of a product or service to the customer. Coordinate sales distribution by establishing sales territories, quotas, and goals and establish training programs for sales representatives. Analyze sales statistics gathered by staff to determine sales potential and inventory requirements and monitor the preferences of customers. (Based on 41 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct research in the field of expertise, prepare project proposals, review project activities and reports, and oversee subject enrollment while ensuring proper informed consent is obtained and documented.
Hire, supervise, or evaluate staff, recruit personnel, and oversee the development or maintenance of staff competence.

Determine scientific or technical goals within guidelines provided by top management and make detailed plans to accomplish these goals, along with developing or implementing policies, standards, or procedures for various projects or operations.
Develop innovative technologies or train staff for their implementation, and provide technical or scientific guidance to technical staff in their respective fields.
Prepare and administer budgets, approve expenditures, and prepare financial reports, along with conducting feasibility studies and cost, material, and efficiency studies.
Consult or negotiate with clients to prepare project specifications and develop client relationships while communicating proposals and project status.
Collaborate with various stakeholders to prepare presentations or reports; ensure compliance with protocols and relevant regulations; and maintain required records of study activity.
Plan or direct research, development, or production activities, and supervise operations or maintenance employees in production activities, including the manufacturing of biofuels.
Conduct, or oversee the conduct of, technical studies for water resources and manage operations, maintenance, or repair of facilities to ensure compliance and improve sustainability.
Monitor activities to ensure compliance with protocols and to maintain adequate production levels while reviewing logs and datasheets for abnormalities in processes and equipment.

Identify and communicate emergencies related to power systems, inspect facilities for normal operation or adherence to safety standards, and respond to issues related to power users or government agencies.
Monitor the operating status of biomass plants, conduct field inspections, and supervise operations to ensure safe and efficient working conditions.
Evaluate power production or demand trends and inspect biomass processes and facilities to identify opportunities for improvement and cost reduction.
Manage investment funds, including selecting investments, developing investment policies, and monitoring financial performance to maximize returns for clients.
Plan and implement marketing strategies and evaluate market opportunities, such as setting financial goals for services and analyzing sales statistics.
Oversee funeral service operations, including scheduling, staff management, and responding to customer needs while ensuring compliance with relevant regulations.
Prepare operational reports and maintain accurate records, including financial statements and performance data, to identify improvement opportunities.
Recruit, train, and evaluate staff while developing operational standards and ensuring compliance with safety regulations in the workplace.
Analyze internal processes and recommend or implement changes to improve operations, including procedural or policy adjustments and cost control measures.
Establish and maintain relationships with stakeholders, provide counsel for families regarding funeral services, and negotiate contracts related to services.
Direct and coordinate activities of workers in various departments and manage administrative support services to enhance overall operational efficiency.

Develop, implement, manage, or evaluate policies and methods to protect personnel against harassment, threats, or violence; develop, arrange for, perform, or assess executive protection activities to reduce security risks; monitor performance of quality control systems to ensure effectiveness and efficiency; analyze quality control test results and provide feedback and interpretation to production management or staff; instruct staff in quality control and analytical procedures; and communicate quality control information to all relevant organizational departments, outside vendors, or contractors.

Confer with marketing and sales departments to define client requirements and expectations; communicate geothermal plant conditions to employees; represent company at trade association meetings to promote products; and resolve customer complaints regarding sales and service.

Create and implement inspection and testing criteria or procedures; direct the tracking of defects, test results, or other regularly reported quality control data; and document testing procedures, methodologies, or criteria.

Develop or manage budgets for geothermal operations; generate and maintain quality control operating budgets; evaluate needs for procurement of funds and investment of surpluses and make appropriate recommendations; and prepare budgets and approve budget expenditures.

Identify quality problems or areas for improvement and recommend solutions; review and update standard operating procedures or quality assurance manuals; and identify critical points in the manufacturing process and specify sampling procedures to be used at these points.

Monitor development of new products to help identify possible problems for mass production; participate in the development of product specifications; and consult with product development personnel on product specifications, such as design, color, or packaging.

Evaluate the financial aspects of product development, such as budgets, expenditures, research and development appropriations, or return-on-investment and profit-loss projections; and analyze the financial details of past, present, and expected operations to identify development opportunities and areas where improvement is needed.

Plan, direct, or coordinate marketing activities or policies to promote products or services; initiate market research studies, or analyze their findings; and conduct market research and analyze industry trends.

Direct the hiring, training, or performance evaluations of marketing or sales staff and oversee their daily activities; and plan and direct staffing, training, and performance evaluations to develop and control sales and service programs.

Analyze sales statistics gathered by staff to determine sales potential and inventory requirements and monitor the preferences of customers; and evaluate new testing and sampling methodologies or technologies to determine usefulness.

Coordinate security operations or activities with public law enforcement, fire and other agencies; develop budgets for security operations; and create or implement security standards, policies, and procedures.

Oversee workers including supervisors, inspectors, or laboratory workers engaged in testing activities; conduct or coordinate audits of company accounts and financial transactions to ensure compliance with state and federal requirements and statutes; and oversee payroll functions, such as maintaining timekeeping information and processing and submitting payroll.

Develop or manage investigation programs, including collection and preservation of video and notes of surveillance processes or investigative interviews; oversee construction and renovation projects to improve efficiency and to ensure that facilities meet environmental, health, and security standards, and comply with government regulations; and direct, coordinate, and review sales and service accounting and record-keeping, as well as receiving and shipping.
Train employees in geothermal plant safety or increase the production of biofuels.

Prepare manuals and train workers in use of new forms, reports, procedures or equipment, according to organizational policy; analyze logistics data, using methods such as data mining, data modeling, or cost or benefit analysis; and report project plans, progress, and results.

Develop pricing strategies, balancing firm objectives and customer satisfaction; determine price schedules and discount rates; and identify, develop, or evaluate marketing strategy, based on knowledge of establishment objectives, market characteristics, and cost and markup factors.
Monitor the facility to ensure that it remains safe, secure, and well-maintained; and oversee the maintenance and repair of machinery, equipment, and electrical and mechanical systems.
Review logistics performance with customers against targets, benchmarks, and service agreements.
Plan study of work problems and procedures, such as organizational change, communications, information flow, integrated production methods, inventory control, or cost analysis.
Protect and control proprietary materials.

Develop an understanding of customers' needs and take actions to ensure that such needs are met. Collaborate with other departments as necessary to meet customer requirements, to take advantage of sales opportunities or, in the case of shortages, to minimize negative impacts on a business.

Direct availability and allocation of materials, supplies, and finished products. Manage the logistical aspects of product life cycles, including coordination or provisioning of samples, and the minimization of obsolescence.

Plan, organize, and execute logistics support activities, such as maintenance planning, repair analysis, and test equipment recommendations. Prepare logistic strategies or conceptual designs for production facilities.

Identify cost-reduction or process-improvement logistic opportunities and recommend improvements to existing or planned logistics processes. Evaluate effectiveness of current or future logistical processes and monitor industry standards, trends, or practices to identify developments in logistics planning or execution.

Provide project management services, including the provision and analysis of technical data. Gather and organize information on problems or procedures and analyze data gathered to develop solutions or alternative methods of proceeding.

Develop or document procedures to minimize or mitigate carbon output resulting from the movement of materials or products. Conduct environmental audits for logistics activities, such as storage, distribution, or transportation.

Evaluate the use of technologies, such as global positioning systems (GPS), radio-frequency identification (RFID), route navigation software, or satellite linkup systems, to improve transportation efficiency. Remotely monitor the flow of vehicles or inventory, using Web-based logistics information systems to track vehicles or containers.

Review global, national, or regional transportation or logistics reports for ways to improve efficiency or minimize the environmental impact of logistics activities. Create models or scenarios to predict the impact of changing circumstances, such as fuel costs, road pricing, energy taxes, or carbon emissions legislation.

Develop specifications for equipment, tools, facility layouts, or material-handling systems. Design comprehensive supply chains that minimize environmental impacts or costs and plan or direct activities, such as sales promotions, that require coordination with other department managers.

Perform managerial duties such as hiring and training employees and overseeing facility needs or requirements. Direct the work of logistics analysts and conduct needs assessments or surveys to determine interest in, or satisfaction with, wellness and fitness programs, events, or services.

Communicate with or monitor service providers, such as ocean carriers, air freight forwarders, global consolidators, customs brokers, or trucking companies. Monitor suppliers to ensure that they efficiently and effectively provide needed goods or services within budgetary limits.

Prepare or validate documentation on automated logistics or maintenance-data reporting or management information systems. Maintain and develop positive business relationships with a customer's key personnel involved in, or directly relevant to, a logistics activity.

Develop logistic metrics, internal analysis tools, or key performance indicators for business units. Utilize computer skills and software to manage Web sites or databases, publish newsletters, or provide webinars.
Track product flow from origin to final delivery and determine logistics support requirements, such as facility details, staffing needs, or safety or maintenance plans.
Design and edit promotional publications, such as brochures. Write or revise standard operating procedures for logistics processes and prepare staff work schedules and assign specific duties.

Prepare or assess budgets related to logistical activities and evaluate financial statements, sales or activity reports, or other performance data to measure productivity or goal achievement or to identify areas needing cost reduction or program improvement.

Identify or develop business rules or standard operating procedures to streamline operating processes. Interview key staff or tour facilities to identify efficiency-improvement, cost-reduction, or service-delivery opportunities.

Provide individual support or counseling in general wellness or nutrition; develop or coordinate fitness and wellness programs or services; oversee health screenings or other preventive measures; organize and oversee events such as organized runs or walks; supervise fitness or wellness specialists, such as fitness instructors or nutritionists; develop fitness or wellness classes, such as yoga or aerobics, ensuring a diversity of class offerings; maintain wellness- and fitness-related schedules, records, or reports; and conduct or facilitate training sessions or seminars for wellness and fitness staff.

Track attendance, participation, or performance data related to wellness events; respond to customer inquiries or complaints about wellness programs; provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations such as workplace violence; and engage in regular communication with staff regarding job responsibilities and performance expectations.

Coordinate theft and fraud investigations involving career criminals or organized group activities; develop relationships with law enforcement to investigate and solve theft or fraud cases; analyze retail data to identify trends in theft or fraud; and assess security needs across locations to ensure appropriate deployment of loss prevention resources.

Monitor compliance systems to ensure their effectiveness; develop or implement plans, processes, or procedures related to compliance; discuss emerging compliance issues with management and employees; ensure proper documentation, implementation, and communication of regulatory policies and procedures; and prepare management reports regarding compliance operations and progress.

Administer first aid in emergency situations; respond to customer, public, or media requests for information about wellness programs or services; verify staff credentials, such as educational and certification requirements; assign tasks and work hours to staff; and ensure that all operational processes are in compliance with health, safety, or hygiene standards.
Prepare or implement budgets and strategic, operational, purchasing, or maintenance plans; calculate and record department expenses and revenue; and establish financial goals for wellness services.

Keep informed regarding pending industry changes, trends, or best practices; and monitor regulatory affairs trends related to environmental issues, ensuring alignment with corporate sustainability or green initiatives.
Verify that all regulatory policies and procedures have been documented, implemented, and communicated; and conduct periodic internal reviews or audits to ensure adherence to compliance procedures.
Identify compliance issues that require follow-up or investigation; and monitor compliance with regulatory requirements and corporate standards, escalating concerns as needed.

Review, negotiate, or approve contracts with contractors providing wellness or fitness services, ensuring quality and adherence to policies or budgets; and develop and maintain partnerships with other organizations to enhance wellness program offerings.
Develop risk management strategies based on compliance assessments; and potentially provide training on compliance-related topics, policies, or procedures.
Prepare reports or presentations to communicate wellness program needs, status, or progress; and provide assistance to internal or external auditors in compliance reviews.

Develop regulatory strategies and implementation plans for the preparation and submission of new wellness programs or services; and ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards.

Demonstrate proper operation of fitness equipment and provide technical support to wellness staff regarding equipment usage; and maintain documentation of compliance activities regarding facility operations.
Coordinate facility schedules to maximize usage and efficiency; and monitor operations to ensure compliance with operational procedures, including physical security and health standards.
Develop or implement marketing strategies for wellness programs, ensuring they align with community health needs and preferences.

Oversee internal reporting systems related to compliance and wellness program activities; and serve as a confidential point of contact for employees to communicate with management regarding compliance-related issues.

Investigate product complaints and prepare documentation pertaining to wellness or fitness services as necessary; and implement or monitor complaint processing systems to ensure effective resolution of complaints.

Formulate or implement regulatory affairs policies and procedures to ensure regulatory compliance is maintained or enhanced, and communicate this information to multiple departments, ensuring correct interpretation.

Direct the preparation and submission of regulatory agency applications, reports, or correspondence and review these materials to ensure timeliness, accuracy, comprehensiveness, or compliance with regulatory standards.
Sell products, services, or memberships, and maintain client databases as part of managing customer relationships.
Train staff in regulatory policies or procedures, and provide continuing education to maintain current industry knowledge.
Evaluate new software publishing systems and confer with regulatory agencies concerning news or updates on electronic publishing of submissions, establishing procedures for publishing documents.
Coordinate internal discoveries and depositions with legal department staff and recommend improvements in loss prevention programs, staffing, scheduling, or training.
Develop and maintain standard operating procedures or local working practices in various organizational departments, focusing on efficiency and compliance.
Supervise surveillance, detection, or criminal processing related to theft and criminal cases, and train loss prevention staff or retail managers on loss control and prevention measures.
Perform or direct inventory investigations in response to shrink results outside of acceptable ranges, and maintain documentation of all loss prevention activity.
Hire or supervise loss prevention staff and advise retail establishments on the development of loss-investigation procedures.
